sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD AND/ OR A NUMBER for each answer.
Questions 11-14: Choose the correct letter A, B, or C. Questions 15-20: Complete the table below. Write ONE WORD AND/ OR A NUMBER for each answer.
sections.passage_or_context:
Matthews Island Holidays
Timetable for Isle of Man Holiday

questions.content: According to the speaker, the company
options: A has been in business for longer than most of its competitors
B arranges holidays to more destinations than its competitors
C has more customers than its competitors

questions.content: Where can customers meet the tour manager before traveling to the Isle of Man?
How many lunches are included in the price of holiday?
Customers have to pay extra for
options: A guaranteeing themselves a larger room

sections.instruction: Questions 21-26: Choose SIX answers from the list A-H below. Questions 27 and 28: Choose the correct letter A, B or C. Questions 29 and 30: Choose TWO letters, A-E.
sections.passage_or_context: Findings of previous research about personality traits of a child based on position in the family.

questions.content: What did findings of previous research claim about the personality traits of a child is likely to have because of their position in the family?
options: 26. a child with much older siblings

questions.content: What do the speakers say about the evidence relating to birth order and academic success?
options: A There is conflicting evidence about whether oldest children perform best in intelligence tests
B There is little doubt that birth order has less influence on academic achievement than socio-economic status
C Some studies have neglected to include important factors such as family side

questions.content: What does Ruth think is surprising about the difference in oldest children’s academic performance
options: A It is mainly thanks to their roles as teachers for their younger siblings
B The advantages they have only lead to a slightly higher level of achievement
C The extra parental attention they receive at a young age makes little difference

questions.content: Which TWO experiences of sibling rivalry do the speakers agree has been valuable for them?
options: B learning to stand up for oneself
C learning to be a good loser

sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.

questions.content:
(31) It provides {{BLANK}} and food for a wide range of species
(32) Its leaves provide {{BLANK}} which is used to make a disinfectant
(33) Mundulla Yellows cause: lime used for making {{BLANK}} was absorbed
(34) Bell-miner Associated Die-back cause: {{BLANK}} feed on eucalyptus leaves
(35) High frequency bushfires have impact on vegetation, resulting in the growth of {{BLANK}}
(36) Mid frequency bushfires result in the growth of eucalyptus forests, because they make more {{BLANK}} available to the trees
(37) Mid frequency bushfires maintain the quality of the {{BLANK}}
(38) Low frequency bushfires result in the growth of {{BLANK}} rainforest
(39) Low frequency bushfires result in the growth of rainforest which is a {{BLANK}} ecosystem
(40) Low frequency bushfires result in the growth of rainforest which is an ideal environment for the {{BLANK}} of the bell-miner
